The most fitting atomic proposition for this is 'patientConnected', representing whether the patient is connected to the breathing circuit. The temporal scope ('when the system is powered on and through start-up and self-test') covers the time before start-up and self-test are completed, suggesting we must ensure that 'patientConnected' does not become true (never satisfied) while those modes are active. The atomic propositions indicating these phases are likely 'StartUpMode' and 'SelfTestMode'. Thus, the structured NL property uses 'while (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode), never satisfy patientConnected' to capture that throughout start-up and self-test, it should never be the case that the patient is connected.
